parts.answer: An answer that makes reference to two of the following improvements and justifications  place a lid on the polystyrene cup and to reduce heat loss  measure the temperature more often and to give a more precise extrapolation (maximum temperature change)  use a pipette / burette (to measure the solution) and less uncertainty (in volume measurement) Or use a thermometer with more gradations / more precise thermometer and to give a more precis e temperature change (1) (1) (1) Ignore just ‘be tter insulation’ Ignore read the temp erature for longer Allow more accurate extrapol ation / line of best fit Ignore just to increase the accuracy Allow to measure the temperature with more precision (i.e with no mention of thermometer). Allow use a digital thermometer Ignore finer zinc powder Ignore use a larger mass / excess of zinc Ignore more concentrated solution If no other mark scored award 1 for two correct improvements of the four given Do not award repeating the experiment Do not award more accurate wei g hin g (2) (Total for Question 1 = 13 marks) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark

 calculation of moles of hydrochloric acid  calculation of moles of MHCO in 250 cm  calculation of relative formula mass of MHCO  calculation of A r of M to 2 decimal places (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation = 13.35 × 0.150 = 0.0020025 / 0.00200 / 2.0025 × 10 ‒ / 2.00 × 10 ‒ (mol) (ans 1) = ans (1) × 10 = 0.020025 / 0.0200 / 2.0025 × 10 ‒ / 2.00 × 10 ‒ (mol) (ans 2) = mass of MHCO = 2.00 = 99.88 (g mol ‒ ) ans (2) 0.020025 (ans 3) = ans (3) - 1 - 12 – 48 = 38.88 (g mol ‒ ) Allow for M2 and M3 mass of MHCO ÷ 10 and then ÷ (ans 1) Allow TE at each stage Allow use of 25cm giving relative formula mass = 53.33 scores (2) for M2 and M3 as 61 cannot be subtracted from it Correct answer with some working scores 4 I g nore SF except 1SF (4) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark  calculation of expe rimental error Example of calculation = 39.1 - 37.52 × 100 = 4.04 / 4.0 / 4 % 39.1 Allow = 39.1 - 37.52 = 1.58 with no further working Allow 4.04 / 4.0 with no working scores 1 Do not award just 4% with no working Do not award = 39.1 - 37.52 × 100 = 4.21 % 37.52 (1) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  calculate the experi mental difference Either  calculation of the range  comment on the range in relation to the value of 39.1 or  calculation of highe st possible value  comment on the value 39.1 being between the highest value and the mi d-point of the range (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Example of calculation 37.52 × 4.50 ÷ 100 = 1.6884 37.52 ± 1.6884 = 35.832 to 39.208 / potassium lies within this range 37.52 + 1.6884 = 39.208 39.1 lies between 37.52 and 39.208 Use of 39.1 ± 1.7595 = (40.8595 to) 37.3405 can score M1 and M2 but cannot score M3. Use of 39.1 – 1.7595 = 37.52 can score M1 and M2 (similar to ‘or’) Allow a correct application of uncertainty to an incorrect value can score M2 and M3 (3) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  increasing the mass of MHCO / decr

An answer that makes refere nce to the following point  measuring cylinder Do not award pipette, beaker Ignore burette Ignore numbers or volume s before the measuring cylinder e.g. 10cm measuring cylinder Do not award measuring cup / jug (1) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  the reaction is exothermic  cyclohexene would be lost because it is volatile / has a low boiling temperature / evaporate (1) (1) Do not award explosive Allow less of the cyclohexene / product produced would be lost / would boil off Allow to prevent / reduce reaction before the distillation experi ment takes place Ignore swirling to mix the reactants Ignore prevents evaporation of volatile liquids Ignore too vigorous Ignore references to sh ifting the position of equilibrium or rate of reaction Ignore increase yield Ignore prevents evaporation / boiling of cyclohexanol / solution (2) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  so the product is not contaminated by the reaction mixture / cyclohexanol be ing transferred to the collecting flask Allow so the reaction mixture / cyclohexanol does not go into the condenser / collecting flask Ignore just to prevent it boiling over Do not award to prevent ignition (1) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  the range starts below the boiling temperature of cyclohexene and finishes below that of water / cyclohexanol  a minimum amount of cyclohexanol / water / phosphoric acid / impurities are distilled across Or so cyclohexene vaporising below the boiling temperature is collected (1) (1) Allow only cyclohexene boils within this range / between 80ºC and 90ºC Ignore statements of the range and boiling temperatures without explanation Allow above this range (more) water (and cyclohexanol) would di stil across / would be collected Allow cyclohexanol / water will remain in the flask / will not be vapourised (2) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  phosphoric((V)) acid / H PO  equation (1) (1) Allow (excess) acid Ignore (excess) H + ions 2H + + CO 32- → CO + H O Allow 2H + + CO 32- → H CO Allow multiples I g nore state s y mbols, even if incorrect (2) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points  diagram of separating fu nnel including a tap and a stopper or capable of being stoppered  two labelled layers (1) (1) Top layer labelled organic layer, bottom layer labelled aqueous layer Ignore cyclohexanol in cyclohexene layer Do not award bottom layer labelled as cyclohexanol but allow labelled as aq ueous layer containing c y clohexanol / water and c y clohexanol (2) aqueous / water / sodium carbonate solution organic / cyclohexene Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following point  ionic compounds / cycl ohexanol / sodium phosphate((V)) Allow (excess) sodium carbonate Allow sodium ions / phosphate((V)) ions / carbonate ions Ignore water Ignore just ‘impurities’ Do not award phosphoric((V)) acid Do not award HCl / hydrochloric acid (1) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark

An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  MgSO  it is an anhydrous (sal t) (and doesn’t react) (1) (1) Allow contains no water / not hydrated Allow reasons why the other five are not suitable M2 depends on M1 (2) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  bromine water / Br (aq) / bromine solution  orange to colourless Or  potassium manganate(VII) / KMnO and sulfuric acid  purple to colourless (1) (1) M2 dependent on M1 or near miss Allow bromine / Br (l) / Br Do not award bromide Allow decolourises bromine / bromine water Allow brown to colourless Allow yellow to colourless If bromine is used do not aw ard yellow / orange to colourless. M2 must be brown/red-brown to colourless Allow potassium permanganate Allow acidified potassium manganate(VII) Do not award hydrochloric acid Allow purple to brown Allow decolourises (2) Numbe r Additional Guidance Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  (addition of) phosphorus pentachloride / phosphorus(V) chloride / PCl  misty / steamy fumes (of HCl) Or  (addition of) sodium  effervescence / bubbles (1) (1) (1) (1) M2 dependent on M1 or near miss Do not award potassium dichromate((VI)) Do not award phosphorus chloride but count as near miss Allow white fumes Ignore litmus paper test on fumes Do not award white gas Allow white solid formed Allow ester formation addition of a carboxylic acid / named carboxylic acid and a strong acid / mineral acid / named strong acid / formula of strong acid (1) sweet / fruit y smell (of ester) (1) (2) Numbe r Additional Guidanc2e Mark An answer that makes refere nce to the following points:  (no because) PCl / Na reacts with water (so would potentially give a false positive test) (1) Allow yes with or without justification if ester formation is used in 3(g)(ii) (1) (Total for Question 3 = 18 marks) (Total for Paper = 50 marks) Pearson Education Limited.
